summary:
The patient’s primary molar 12 has an immature root and vital pulp, making revascularization appropriate as a conservative approach to promote healing and maintain dental development. Meanwhile, the adjacent mature second premolar 11 shows signs of infection with radiolucency and requires root canal treatment due to established periradicular pathology. 

Note: This summary focuses only on clinically significant decisions based directly on the case details provided.
